time_period: 476-1000 CE (Fall of Rome to Early Medieval Ireland)
key_themes: Fall of Western Roman Empire (476 CE)
Irish monastic scholarship as knowledge refuge
Pattern Eye custody chain through collapse
Clonmacnoise and Irish golden age of learning

seven_keys_status.Pattern Eye: Passed to 4-year-old Ciarán during Rome's fall; carried through Dark Ages to Irish monasteries

major_events: Ciarán receives Pattern Eye at age 4 in Ravenna

network_evolution.key_insight: Dark Ages require knowledge preservation in isolated, protected centers - Irish monasteries become the refuge
network_evolution.pattern_recognition: Ciarán has genetic memory/intuition even at age 4 - 'the knowing came from somewhere deep inside, older than his four years'
network_evolution.defensive_strategy: Scatter knowledge across remote Irish monasteries where Roman collapse can't reach; use monastic system for protection
